title: Two Sum
description: Given an array of integers nums and an integer target, return indices of the two numbers that add up to target.
hints: Think about what complement you need for each number.
Use a hash map to store seen values.
For each num, check if target-num exists in the map.
solution_approach: Use a hash map to store each number's index. For each element, check if the complement exists.

title: Best Time to Buy and Sell Stock
description: Given an array prices where prices[i] is the price on the ith day, find the maximum profit from one transaction.
hints: Track the minimum price seen so far.
At each step, compute profit = current - min_so_far.
Return the maximum profit found.
solution_approach: Track minimum price and maximum profit in a single pass.

title: Product of Array Except Self
description: Given an integer array nums, return an array where answer[i] is the product of all elements except nums[i], without using division.
hints: Think about prefix and suffix products.
First pass: compute prefix products left to right.
Second pass: multiply by suffix products right to left.
solution_approach: Use prefix and suffix product arrays combined into one pass.

title: Container With Most Water
description: Given n non-negative integers representing heights, find two lines that form a container holding the most water.
hints: Use two pointers at both ends.
Move the shorter pointer inward.
Track the maximum area.
solution_approach: Two pointer approach — move the shorter line inward to potentially find taller lines.

title: Reverse Linked List
description: Given the head of a singly linked list, reverse the list and return the reversed list.
hints: Use three pointers: prev, current, next.
At each step, reverse the current node's pointer.
Move all pointers one step forward.
solution_approach: Iterative reversal using prev, curr, next pointers.

title: Merge Two Sorted Lists
description: Merge two sorted linked lists into one sorted list.
hints: Compare heads of both lists.
Pick the smaller one and recurse.
Handle when one list is exhausted.
solution_approach: Use a dummy head and compare nodes one by one.

title: Linked List Cycle
description: Given head, determine if the linked list has a cycle.
hints: Use two pointers at different speeds.
If they meet, there's a cycle.
If fast reaches null, no cycle.
solution_approach: Floyd's cycle detection with fast and slow pointers.

title: LRU Cache
description: Design a data structure for Least Recently Used (LRU) cache with O(1) get and put.

examples.input: LRUCache(2), put(1,1), put(2,2), get(1), put(3,3), get(2)

hints: Combine a hash map with a doubly linked list.
Map gives O(1) lookup, list gives O(1) removal.
Most recent items go to the front.
solution_approach: Hash map + doubly linked list for O(1) operations.

title: Valid Parentheses
description: Given a string containing just (){}[], determine if the input string is valid.
hints: Use a stack to track opening brackets.
When closing bracket found, check top of stack.
Stack must be empty at the end.
solution_approach: Stack-based matching of opening and closing brackets.

title: Min Stack
description: Design a stack that supports push, pop, top, and retrieving the minimum element in O(1).

examples.input: push(-2), push(0), push(-3), getMin(), pop(), top(), getMin()

constraints: Methods will be called at most 3*10^4 times
hints: Store the current minimum alongside each element.
Use an auxiliary stack tracking minimums.
Each push records the min at that point.
solution_approach: Use an auxiliary min-stack that tracks the minimum at each level.

title: Daily Temperatures
description: Given temperatures, return how many days you must wait for a warmer temperature.
hints: Use a monotonic decreasing stack.
Store indices, not values.
When a warmer day is found, pop and calculate difference.
solution_approach: Monotonic decreasing stack storing indices.

title: Largest Rectangle in Histogram
description: Given an array of heights representing a histogram, find the area of the largest rectangle.
hints: Use a stack to track bar indices.
Pop when current height is less than stack top.
Calculate width using stack boundaries.
solution_approach: Use monotonic stack to find left and right boundaries for each bar.

title: Maximum Depth of Binary Tree
description: Given the root of a binary tree, return its maximum depth.
hints: Think recursively: depth = 1 + max(left, right).
Base case: null node has depth 0.
BFS level-by-level also works.
solution_approach: Recursive DFS: return 1 + max(left depth, right depth).

title: Validate Binary Search Tree
description: Given the root of a binary tree, determine if it is a valid BST.
hints: Each node must be within a valid range.
Pass min/max bounds recursively.
In-order traversal should be strictly increasing.
solution_approach: Recursive validation passing valid (min, max) range to each node.

title: Binary Tree Level Order Traversal
description: Given the root, return the level order traversal as nested arrays.
hints: Use a queue (BFS).
Process all nodes at current level before moving on.
Track level size at each iteration.
solution_approach: BFS with queue, processing one level at a time.

title: Serialize and Deserialize Binary Tree
description: Design an algorithm to serialize and deserialize a binary tree.
hints: Use preorder traversal for serialization.
Mark null nodes with a sentinel value.
Use a queue/index to deserialize.
solution_approach: Preorder DFS with null markers for serialization.

title: Number of Islands
description: Given an m x n grid of '1's (land) and '0's (water), count the number of islands.
hints: Iterate through each cell.
When you find a '1', run DFS/BFS to mark the island.
Count how many times you start a new traversal.
solution_approach: DFS/BFS flood fill from each unvisited land cell.

title: Clone Graph
description: Given a reference to a node in a connected undirected graph, return a deep copy.
hints: Use a hash map to track cloned nodes.
DFS/BFS to traverse the original graph.
For each neighbor, clone if not already cloned.
solution_approach: DFS with hash map mapping original nodes to clones.

title: Course Schedule
description: Determine if you can finish all courses given prerequisites (cycle detection).
hints: Build an adjacency list from prerequisites.
Detect cycles using DFS with coloring.
If no cycle exists, courses can be completed.
solution_approach: Topological sort using DFS cycle detection or Kahn's algorithm.

title: Word Ladder
description: Given two words and a dictionary, find the length of the shortest transformation sequence.
hints: Use BFS for shortest path.
At each step, try changing one letter.
Use a set for O(1) word lookup.
solution_approach: BFS level-by-level, generating neighbors by changing one letter at a time.

title: Climbing Stairs
description: You can climb 1 or 2 steps. How many distinct ways to climb n steps?
hints: This is a Fibonacci variant.
dp[i] = dp[i-1] + dp[i-2].
Base cases: dp[1]=1, dp[2]=2.
solution_approach: Bottom-up DP equivalent to Fibonacci sequence.

title: Coin Change
description: Given coins and an amount, return the fewest coins needed. Return -1 if not possible.
hints: Define dp[i] = min coins for amount i.
For each coin, dp[i] = min(dp[i], dp[i-coin]+1).
Initialize dp[0]=0, rest=infinity.
solution_approach: Bottom-up DP: dp[amount] = min over all coins of dp[amount-coin]+1.

title: Longest Increasing Subsequence
description: Given an integer array, return the length of the longest strictly increasing subsequence.
hints: Define dp[i] = LIS ending at index i.
For each i, check all j < i where nums[j] < nums[i].
Optimize to O(n log n) with patience sorting.
solution_approach: DP with binary search optimization using a tails array.

title: Edit Distance
description: Given two strings, return the minimum number of operations (insert, delete, replace) to convert one to the other.
hints: Use a 2D DP table.
dp[i][j] = min ops to convert word1[:i] to word2[:j].
Three choices: insert, delete, replace.
solution_approach: 2D DP where dp[i][j] considers insert, delete, and replace operations.

title: Binary Search
description: Given a sorted array and target, return the index or -1.
hints: Use two pointers: left and right.
Compare mid element with target.
Narrow the search space by half each time.
solution_approach: Classic binary search with left, right, mid pointers.

title: Search in Rotated Sorted Array
description: Search for a target in a rotated sorted array.
hints: Find which half is sorted.
Check if target lies in the sorted half.
Adjust search boundaries accordingly.
solution_approach: Modified binary search checking which half is sorted.

title: Find Minimum in Rotated Sorted Array
description: Find the minimum element in a rotated sorted array.
hints: Compare mid with right boundary.
If mid > right, min is in right half.
Otherwise min is in left half (including mid).
solution_approach: Binary search comparing mid with right endpoint.

title: Median of Two Sorted Arrays
description: Given two sorted arrays, find the median of the merged array in O(log(m+n)).
hints: Binary search on the shorter array.
Find correct partition point.
Median is derived from boundary elements.
solution_approach: Binary search on shorter array to find correct partition.

title: Kth Largest Element in an Array
description: Find the kth largest element in an unsorted array.
hints: Use a min-heap of size k.
Push elements; if heap > k, pop smallest.
Top of heap is kth largest.
solution_approach: Maintain a min-heap of size k.

title: Merge K Sorted Lists
description: Merge k sorted linked lists into one sorted list.
hints: Use a min-heap with one node from each list.
Pop smallest, push its next node.
Continue until heap is empty.
solution_approach: Min-heap holding one node from each list, always extracting minimum.

title: Find Median from Data Stream
description: Design a data structure that supports addNum and findMedian efficiently.
hints: Use two heaps: max-heap for lower half, min-heap for upper.
Balance sizes after each insertion.
Median is derived from heap tops.
solution_approach: Two heaps: max-heap for lower half, min-heap for upper half.

title: Top K Frequent Elements
description: Given an integer array and k, return the k most frequent elements.
hints: Count frequencies with a hash map.
Use a min-heap of size k.
Or use bucket sort by frequency.
solution_approach: Count frequencies, then use min-heap or bucket sort.

title: Subsets
description: Given an integer array of unique elements, return all possible subsets.
hints: At each index, choose to include or exclude.
Build subsets incrementally.
Use backtracking with a path array.
solution_approach: Backtracking: at each element, include it or skip it.

title: Permutations
description: Given an array of distinct integers, return all permutations.
hints: Swap elements to generate permutations.
Use a visited set or swap-based approach.
Backtrack after each recursive call.
solution_approach: Backtracking with swap or visited-set approach.

title: N-Queens
description: Place n queens on an n x n chessboard so no two queens attack each other.
hints: Place queens row by row.
Track columns and diagonals under attack.
Backtrack when no valid position exists.
solution_approach: Row-by-row placement with column and diagonal tracking.

title: Word Search
description: Given an m x n grid and a word, return true if the word exists in the grid.
hints: DFS from each cell matching first character.
Mark cells as visited during exploration.
Backtrack by unmarking cells.
solution_approach: DFS backtracking from each starting cell.

title: Jump Game
description: Given an array where nums[i] is max jump length from i, determine if you can reach the last index.
hints: Track the farthest reachable index.
At each step, update the farthest reach.
If current index > farthest, return false.
solution_approach: Greedy: track maximum reachable index.

title: Merge Intervals
description: Given an array of intervals, merge all overlapping intervals.
hints: Sort intervals by start time.
If current overlaps with previous, merge.
Otherwise start a new interval.
solution_approach: Sort by start time, then merge overlapping intervals greedily.

title: Task Scheduler
description: Given tasks and cooldown n, find minimum intervals to finish all tasks.
hints: Count frequencies of each task.
Most frequent task determines the frame.
Fill gaps with other tasks.
solution_approach: Greedy based on most frequent task's idle slots.

title: Gas Station
description: Given gas and cost arrays for a circular route, find starting station index or -1.
hints: If total gas < total cost, impossible.
Track running tank; reset start when tank < 0.
The reset point is the answer.
solution_approach: Greedy: if total gas >= total cost, there exists a solution. Reset start on negative tank.

title: Implement Trie
description: Implement a trie with insert, search, and startsWith methods.
hints: Each node has children map and is_end flag.
Insert: walk or create nodes per character.
Search: walk nodes, check is_end at last char.
solution_approach: Trie node with children dict and end-of-word flag.

title: Word Search II
description: Given a board and a list of words, return all words that can be found in the board.
hints: Build a trie from the word list.
DFS from each cell using the trie.
Prune branches that don't match any word prefix.
solution_approach: Build trie from words, then DFS backtracking on board using trie for pruning.

title: Design Add and Search Words
description: Design a data structure supporting addWord and search (with '.' wildcards).
hints: Use a trie for storage.
On '.', branch to all children.
Use DFS for wildcard matching.
solution_approach: Trie with DFS search handling '.' as wildcard.

title: Replace Words
description: Given a dictionary of roots and a sentence, replace each word with its shortest root.
hints: Build a trie from the dictionary.
For each word, find shortest prefix match.
Replace word with the root if found.
solution_approach: Build trie from roots, search for shortest prefix match per word.

title: Single Number
description: Every element appears twice except one. Find the single one.
hints: XOR of a number with itself is 0.
XOR of a number with 0 is itself.
XOR all elements; duplicates cancel out.
solution_approach: XOR all elements — duplicates cancel, leaving the single number.

title: Number of 1 Bits
description: Return the number of '1' bits (Hamming weight) in an unsigned integer.
hints: Use n & (n-1) to clear the lowest set bit.
Count iterations until n becomes 0.
Or use built-in popcount.
solution_approach: Brian Kernighan's algorithm: n & (n-1) clears lowest set bit.
time_complexity: O(k) where k = number of 1 bits

title: Counting Bits
description: Given an integer n, return an array of the number of 1's in binary for 0 to n.
hints: Right shift removes last bit.
Last bit contributes 0 or 1.
solution_approach: DP: bits[i] = bits[i >> 1] + (i & 1).

title: Reverse Bits
description: Reverse bits of a given 32-bit unsigned integer.
hints: Process one bit at a time from right.
Shift result left, add current bit.
Repeat 32 times.
solution_approach: Iterate 32 times: extract last bit, shift into result.
